refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for accurate energy monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to optimize fuel/electricity usage.
DP3: Develop a feedback control system to adjust operations based on energy efficiency KPIs.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for accurate energy monitoring.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency on vehicle sensors
Control algorithm dependency on feedback from energy monitoring
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically precedes DP2 and DP3, enabling modular design with manageable coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate energy sensors on vehicle.
Risk that sensor data latency may affect real-time control effectiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing DP2 and DP3 further in next level to reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including energy sensors and vehicle control systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Energy consumption data sent from Vehicle to NGHE Solution for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operational commands received from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for optimized control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as energy sensors and control logic are physically on Vehicle; low diffusion risk.
Contains higher-level monitoring and analysis; moderate containment but higher diffusion risk if DP logic is split.
Minimal containment; primarily remote control, not energy monitoring.
No containment; unrelated to energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring and control DP is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and ensure real-time data accuracy.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with NGHE Solution for KPI aggregation.
Risk of insufficient modularity if control algorithms are tightly coupled with physical sensors.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting control algorithms into NGHE Solution module if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and measurable; selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if control logic is not well partitioned between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for DP; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring telemetry data and comparing against energy efficiency targets.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy monitoring DP to Vehicle IM; interfaces to NGHE Solution are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose control-related DP in next level to reduce diffusion risk.
Clarify interface protocols between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of control-related DP and clearer interface definitions are needed to reduce diffusion risk and improve modularity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency targets.

revise.why: To decompose control algorithms and feedback mechanisms for better modularity and reduced diffusion risk.

assumptions: Energy sensors on Vehicle provide accurate and timely data.
Telemetry and tasking interfaces are reliable and secure.
open_risks: Latency in sensor data affecting control responsiveness.
Potential complexity in interface management between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy and latency test results.
Interface protocol specifications and performance metrics.
